name: Immer (Immutable State Updates)

performance.notes: Proxy-based; near zero-cost for small updates; scales with object size

agent_readiness.notes: Immutable state management using JavaScript Proxy. produce(baseState, draft => { draft.user.name = 'John' }) returns new immutable state without mutation syntax complexity. Structural sharing for performance. Works with Redux Toolkit (uses Immer internally), Zustand, and standalone. Use-immer hook for React. Patches for undo/redo. TypeScript types. Solves React state update boilerplate. Most readable approach to immutable state in JS.

gotchas: Proxy-based — old browsers need polyfill (IE11 not supported)
Cannot use draft values outside produce()
Large nested objects can have performance overhead
Freezing behavior in development mode differs from production
best_when: Complex nested state updates in React applications
Redux reducers (Redux Toolkit uses it by default)
Any immutable update that would otherwise require spread operators
avoid_when: Simple flat state updates (spread operators sufficient)
Performance-critical tight loops with state updates
summary: Immer lets you write mutable code that produces immutable state. produce(state, draft => { draft.value = 'new' }) — write natural mutations, get immutable updates. Proxy-based structural sharing. Used internally by Redux Toolkit. Works with Zustand. TypeScript types. Patches for undo/redo. Most readable approach to immutable nested state updates in JavaScript/React applications.
